root: ^Not downward — there is no down here. Not upward, or sideways. He falls in every direction at once, and in no direction at all.

^The void screams around him. Matter and un-matter collide in endless, purposeless war. Fragments of half-formed worlds tumble past — a shard of rock that dissolves into fire, fire that becomes water, water that freezes into nothing.
^His wings beat against something that is not air. Each stroke carries him a league, or an inch, or nowhere at all.
^This is Chaos. The eldest realm. Older than Heaven, older than Hell. The raw stuff of creation, never shaped, never ruled, never tamed.
^And Satan is utterly, terribly alone in it.
^Press onward through the maelstrom, wings spread wide against the void.
^Search for any landmark, any fixed point in this formless sea.
^Pause. Listen. Even chaos must have its own logic.

c-0: ^Satan drives forward with the fury that once stormed Heaven's gates. His wings crack against colliding elements — a gust of searing heat, then absolute cold, then a pressure that would crush mountains.
^But the void does not care about endurance. A wave of un-matter strikes him sideways, sending him tumbling through a region where sound becomes visible and light has weight.
c-1: ^Satan wheels through the formless dark, searching. His eyes — which have seen the throne of God and the fires of Hell — find nothing to fix upon.
^No horizon. No ground. No sky. Only the endless churning of elements at war with themselves.
^Then — there. Far off, or impossibly close, a faint turbulence with a pattern to it. Not order, exactly, but something less than perfect disorder.
c-2: ^Satan stills his wings and lets the void carry him. An act of faith — though he would never call it that.
^And in the silence of his own stillness, he hears it: a rhythm beneath the chaos. Not a heartbeat, not a melody, but something like the memory of a pulse. The void breathes, after a fashion.
^Where it exhales, matter flies apart. Where it inhales, things briefly coalesce. If he times his flight to that ancient respiration...

the_deep: ^Hours pass. Or centuries. Time has no dominion here.
^Satan's wings are battered, half-broken. The fires that once wreathed him gutter and flare unpredictably. He has crossed regions of pure darkness and regions of blinding, meaningless light.
^Upon a throne — if such a word can describe a heap of collapsing and reforming matter — sit two vast presences. One is darkness given weight. The other is shadow given voice.
^Chaos and Night. The ancient monarchs of the void.
^The voice is like an avalanche of breaking worlds. Satan steadies himself against it.
^"I am Satan, Prince of Hell. I seek passage to the world above."
^"I am an enemy of the one who stole your kingdom to build His creation."
^Bow before the ancient powers. Even pride must bend before Chaos itself.

c-0: ^PRINCE OF NOTHING. YOUR HELL IS A CARVED-OUT WOUND IN OUR FLESH. YOUR HEAVEN — ANOTHER. AND NOW THIS "EARTH" HE HAS MADE — A THIRD THEFT FROM OUR DOMAIN.
^The darkness beside Chaos stirs. Night speaks, and her voice is the absence of sound shaped into words.
^Every world He builds, He builds from us. Every star, every stone, every living thing — stolen matter, shaped against its nature, forced into form.
c-1: ^Chaos leans forward. The motion sends shockwaves through the void, and Satan tumbles before righting himself.
^Night's voice curls through the emptiness like smoke.
^The Maker who carves worlds from our body. Who imposes His order on our freedom. Who calls our ancient kingdom mere "confusion" and dares to shape it to His will.
c-2: ^Satan lowers his wings. It costs him — oh, how it costs him — but he bows his head before the ancient powers.
^EVEN THE PROUD ONE KNEELS BEFORE THE ELDEST DARK.
^Night regards him with what might be curiosity.
^Speak, fallen angel. You did not come here to kneel. What do you want?

the_proposition: ^Satan sees his opening. Even here, at the bottom of all things, there is politics. There is leverage.
^I seek the new world — the Earth that God has made. Not to admire it. To corrupt it. To ruin His newest work as He ruined my war in Heaven.
^Chaos shifts on his impossible throne. Matter collapses and reforms around him.
^"If I succeed, His creation falls to ruin — and the matter returns to you. Chaos reclaims what order stole."
^"I ask only passage. Point me toward the world of Man, and I will trouble your realm no further."
^"We share an enemy. That should be enough."

c-0: ^The effect is immediate. Chaos surges upward, and for a moment the entire void seems to pulse with something almost like excitement.
^We will show you the way, Satan. Not from love — we love nothing. But from hunger. We want our kingdom whole again.
c-1: ^Night regards him with ancient, depthless eyes.
^Simple passage? Through our realm? You ask as if we are innkeepers, and this a road.
^LET HIM PASS. IF HIS MISCHIEF BRINGS RUIN TO THE MAKER'S TOY, THEN PERHAPS SOME CRUMB OF STOLEN MATTER FALLS BACK TO US. IT IS A SMALL WAGER FOR A GREAT RETURN.
c-2: ^A SHARED ENEMY. YES. THE MAKER CARVED HEAVEN FROM OUR SUBSTANCE. THEN HELL. AND NOW THIS FRAGILE BAUBLE HE CALLS EARTH.
^Three thefts. Three wounds in the body of the void.
^Chaos churns. Night deepens. And between them, a decision is made — ancient, wordless, absolute.
^Go, fallen one. We will not hinder you. Let the Maker's creation be unmade.

the_passage: ^Chaos gestures — a sweep of formless power — and the void parts.
^Not a road. Not a path. But a direction. For the first time since entering this realm, Satan can feel which way is up. Which way leads out.
^Far, impossibly far, but visible now — a glimmer. Faint as a single candle seen across an ocean. The light of the created world.
^Satan spreads his broken wings and flies.

^The journey is agony. Even with Chaos's grudging aid, the void fights him. He plunges through regions of fire and ice, through pockets where gravity inverts, through spaces where his own thoughts become visible and mocking.
^He falls a thousand leagues, then catches himself. He climbs, only to be dashed down again. His immortal body — which laughed at hellfire — is battered, torn, nearly broken.
^Draw on the old fury — the rage that defied God Himself. Let it burn through the void.
^Think of the prize ahead. Man. God's beloved. Soon to be God's grief.
^Remember what was lost. Heaven's light. The morning star's glory. Fight for the memory of what you were.

c-0: ^The rage comes easy. It always has. Satan was born for rebellion — or made for it, which amounts to the same thing.
^He roars into the void, and the void flinches. His wings beat with the fury of the first war, when he stood against the Almighty and dared to say No.
^The chaos parts before him. Not in respect — in self-preservation. Even the formless deep knows danger when it sees it.
c-1: ^Satan fixes his mind on what lies ahead. Not what lies behind — the lost throne, the burning fall, the lake of fire. Ahead.
^Man. God's new creation. Made in His image, set in a garden of delights, given dominion over a world. Everything Satan lost, given freely to creatures of clay.
^The injustice of it carries him forward like a dark wind.
c-2: ^For one moment, Satan allows himself to remember. Not the war, not the fall — before that. The light.
^He was Lucifer once. The morning star. The brightest of all God's angels. He remembers what it felt like to stand in that radiance, to be made of it, to shine.
^The memory hurts worse than any wound Chaos can inflict. But it drives him onward, because what he was, he can never be again — and someone must pay for that loss.

emergence: ^And then — suddenly, impossibly — he is through.
^The chaos falls away behind him like a nightmare upon waking. The roaring silence gives way to true silence, and then to something he has not heard in ages.
^The music of the spheres. The gentle, terrible harmony of God's creation, turning in its ordered dance.
^Satan hangs in the void between worlds. Below him — impossibly far, impossibly beautiful — a blue-green sphere turns slowly in the light of a young sun.
^He stares at it. And for one moment — just one — something moves in him that might be wonder. Or grief. Or the memory of what beauty meant, before he declared war on its maker.
^Then the moment passes. His jaw sets. His eyes harden.
^Satan folds his battered wings and begins his long descent toward the world of Man.
